ops.title: Per-occurrence rewrite primitives rollout (cc-transcript → capt-hook → ccx pack)
ops.description: Fix the getaway-session guard misfire (whole-line replace from cl.primary + quote-frozen tilde + tilth silent read→search). Approved plan: /Users/yasyf/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-01/plans/what-went-wrong-here-enumerated-sifakis.md. Spans three repos with two hard release gates: cc-transcript (Command.span/Occurrence/splice), capt-hook (rewrite_command_occurrences + FileFixture home/{file} harness), cc-context (Go fail-fast ccx code read + pack redesign + guard migrations). Never import unreleased symbols across the train (ebf5434 pattern). Gate-1 caveat found on contact: cc-transcript HEAD carries an in-flight breaking inversion (feat(py)!/fix(parser)! commits since v13.1.0) — release strategy needs a decision before tagging.

ops.text: cc-transcript release ships Command.span + CommandLine.splice/occurrences with the Rust parity golden untouched
capt-hook v9.14.0 ships rewrite_command_occurrences + Occurrence re-export + FileFixture(home=True)/{file} harness
cc-context: ccx code read fails exit-3 on unresolvable paths on CLI+MCP+exec lanes (no silent tilth search)
cc-context pack released (>=0.6.0) with cat_rewrites per-occurrence + expanduser/stat/is_large gate and sed/headtail/find/ls/vcs/web migrations
incident replay: the three-segment cat line executes untouched under the released pack; a large-file variant splices only its own segment
